questions.question: Which of these hands ranks the highest?

explanations.A: Correct. Among these four, a flush is the strongest hand.
explanations.B: A straight beats three of a kind and two pair but loses to flushes and full houses.
explanations.C: Two pair is well down the chart, below straights and flushes.
explanations.D: Three of a kind loses to straights and flushes.

questions.question: Which of these hands ranks the lowest?

explanations.A: One pair beats high card, so it is not the lowest here.
explanations.B: Correct. High card is the weakest possible poker hand.
explanations.C: Two pair is stronger than both one pair and high card.
explanations.D: A straight is the strongest of these four.

questions.question: Which hand beats a flush?

explanations.A: A straight loses to a flush.
explanations.B: Two pair is far below a flush.
explanations.C: Correct. A full house is the next rank above a flush.
explanations.D: Three of a kind loses to a flush.

questions.question: Which hand beats a full house?

explanations.A: A flush loses to a full house.
explanations.B: A straight is well below a full house.
explanations.C: Two pair cannot beat a full house.
explanations.D: Correct. Four of a kind is the next rank above a full house.

questions.question: Between a straight and a flush, which wins?

explanations.A: Correct. A flush outranks a straight.
explanations.B: A straight is one rank below a flush.
explanations.C: Different hand ranks do not tie.
explanations.D: A flush is by definition suited; it simply outranks the straight.

questions.question: Between two pair and three of a kind, which wins?

explanations.A: Two pair loses to three of a kind.
explanations.B: Correct. Three of a kind outranks two pair.
explanations.C: These are different ranks and do not tie.
explanations.D: Even ace-high two pair loses to the lowest three of a kind.

questions.question: Between one pair and high card, which wins?

explanations.A: High card is the weakest hand and loses to a pair.
explanations.B: A pair always outranks high card.
explanations.C: Correct. Any pair beats the best high-card hand.
explanations.D: Even ace-high loses to the lowest pair.

questions.question: What exactly is a royal flush?
options: Any five cards of one suit
Four aces with any fifth card
A-K-Q-J-10 all of the same suit

explanations.A: That describes an ordinary flush, not a royal flush.
explanations.B: That is four of a kind, not a royal flush.
explanations.C: Mixed suits make only a straight (Broadway), not a flush.
explanations.D: Correct. The ten-to-ace straight flush is the royal flush.

questions.question: What is the best possible hand in Texas Hold'em?

explanations.A: Correct. The royal flush (ace-high straight flush) cannot be beaten.
explanations.B: Quad aces lose to any straight flush.
explanations.C: A full house loses to flushes, quads, and straight flushes.
explanations.D: An ace-high flush still loses to straights flushes and higher categories.

questions.question: Which five cards form the 'wheel'?

explanations.A: That is Broadway, the ace-high straight.
explanations.B: Correct. The wheel uses the ace as a low card below the two.
explanations.C: That is a ten-high straight, not the wheel.
explanations.D: That is a nine-high straight, not the wheel.

questions.question: Which five cards form 'Broadway'?

explanations.A: That is the wheel, the lowest straight.
explanations.B: That is a king-high straight, not Broadway.
explanations.C: Correct. Broadway is the ace-high straight, the highest straight.
explanations.D: The gap at the ten means this isn't even a straight.

questions.question: What is the common slang for a full house?

explanations.A: The wheel is the A-2-3-4-5 straight.
explanations.B: Broadway is the ace-high straight.
explanations.C: 'The nuts' means the best possible hand, not specifically a full house.
explanations.D: Correct. A full house is often called a 'boat' or 'full boat'.

questions.question: A kicker matters when:
options: Two players have the same rank of hand
A player has a straight flush

explanations.A: Correct. Side cards (kickers) break ties between equal-ranked hands.
explanations.B: A straight flush is decided by its top card, not a kicker.
explanations.C: Pot size has nothing to do with kickers.
explanations.D: Kickers are compared at showdown, not preflop.

questions.question: A made poker hand always consists of exactly how many cards?

explanations.A: Two is the number of hole cards, not a full hand.
explanations.B: Correct. The best five-card combination determines hand strength.
explanations.C: Seven cards are available in Hold'em, but only five form the hand.
explanations.D: A poker hand is five cards, not six.

questions.question: In Texas Hold'em you may use how many of your two hole cards?

explanations.A: You may also use one or zero hole cards.
explanations.B: You can use zero, one, or two hole cards.
explanations.C: Correct. You make the best five-card hand from any mix of hole and community cards.
explanations.D: Hold'em deals only two hole cards, not four.

questions.question: A flush requires how many cards of the same suit?

explanations.A: Four of a suit is only a flush draw, not a made flush.
explanations.B: Three suited cards is far from a flush.
explanations.C: A poker hand is five cards; you never need six for a flush.
explanations.D: Correct. Exactly five cards of one suit make a flush.

questions.question: How many distinct hand-ranking categories are there in standard poker?

explanations.A: Correct. From high card up to royal flush there are ten categories.
explanations.B: There are more than five; for example pair, two pair, trips, straight, flush, and beyond.
explanations.C: Seven undercounts the categories.
explanations.D: Thirteen is the number of card ranks, not hand categories.

questions.question: A full house is made of:
options: Three of one rank plus a pair of another
Four of a kind plus a kicker

explanations.A: Two pair is a weaker, different hand.
explanations.B: Correct. Trips plus a pair equals a full house.
explanations.C: That is quads, a stronger and different hand.
explanations.D: That is a straight.

questions.question: Four of a kind plus its required fifth card totals:
options: Five cards (the quads plus one kicker)

explanations.A: A complete poker hand is always five cards.
explanations.B: Only five cards form the hand.
explanations.C: Correct. The hand is the four matching cards and a single kicker.
explanations.D: You never use eight cards in a five-card hand.

questions.question: Two pair means:
options: The same rank appears three times
Two players each hold a pair
A pair plus a flush draw
Two different ranks are each paired

explanations.A: That is three of a kind.
explanations.B: Two pair refers to one player's five cards, not two players.
explanations.C: A draw is not part of a made hand.
explanations.D: Correct. For example, kings and sevens with a side card.

questions.question: Which of these is exactly one pair?

explanations.A: Correct. A single pair of kings with three unrelated kickers.
explanations.B: That is two pair.
explanations.C: That is three of a kind.
explanations.D: That is a straight, not a pair.

questions.question: Board: 9♠ 7♥ 3♣ 9♣ 4♦. You hold 6♠ 3♦. What is your best five-card hand?

explanations.C: Four of a kind is not the best holding here; the strongest five-card hand is two pair, nines and threes.
explanations.D: High card is not the best holding here; the strongest five-card hand is two pair, nines and threes.

questions.question: Board: 8♣ 9♣ 5♠ Q♣ 7♦. You hold 5♥ 4♠. What is your best five-card hand?

questions.question: Between four of a kind and a full house, which wins?

explanations.A: A full house loses to four of a kind.
explanations.B: Different ranks; no tie.
explanations.C: The hand rank decides, not a single ace.
explanations.D: Correct. Quads outrank a full house.

questions.question: Between a straight flush and four of a kind, which wins?
options: Four of a kind with aces

explanations.A: Correct. A straight flush is the second-highest category, above quads.
explanations.B: Quads lose to a straight flush.
explanations.C: Different ranks; no tie.
explanations.D: Even quad aces lose to any straight flush.

questions.question: List from weakest to strongest: flush, straight, full house.

explanations.A: A flush actually beats a straight, so this order is wrong.
explanations.B: Correct. Straight is weakest, then flush, then full house.
explanations.C: This reverses the order; a full house is strongest.
explanations.D: A full house beats a flush, so this is wrong.

questions.question: List from weakest to strongest: two pair, trips, straight.
options: Three of a kind, two pair, straight
Straight, two pair, three of a kind
Two pair, three of a kind, straight
Two pair, straight, three of a kind

explanations.A: Three of a kind beats two pair, so trips is not weakest.
explanations.B: A straight is the strongest of the three, not the weakest.
explanations.C: Correct. Two pair, then trips, then straight.
explanations.D: A straight beats three of a kind, so this order is wrong.

questions.question: Which of these is a straight flush?

explanations.A: All diamonds, but the gap at the ten makes it just a flush, not a straight flush.
explanations.B: The nine of hearts breaks the flush; this is only a straight.
explanations.C: That is a full house, not a straight flush.
explanations.D: Correct. Five consecutive clubs form a straight flush.

questions.question: A straight flush requires:
options: Five consecutive ranks, all the same suit
Five of the same suit in any order
Five consecutive ranks of any suits
Any four-card flush plus a straight card

explanations.A: Correct. Both the straight and the flush conditions must hold.
explanations.B: That is just a flush; the ranks must also be consecutive.
explanations.C: That is just a straight; all five must share a suit.
explanations.D: Only the made five-card hand counts; four-flushes don't qualify.

questions.question: In a straight, the ace can be used as:
options: Either the highest card or the lowest card
A wild card of any rank

explanations.A: The ace can also play low in the wheel (A-2-3-4-5).
explanations.B: Correct. Ace is high in Broadway and low in the wheel.
explanations.C: The ace also plays high in Broadway (A-K-Q-J-10).
explanations.D: There are no wild cards in Hold'em; the ace is fixed at high or low.

questions.question: Is Q-K-A-2-3 a valid straight?
options: Yes, the ace connects king and two
Yes, any five cards in a row count
No, straights cannot wrap around the ace
Only if they are the same suit

explanations.A: No — a straight cannot 'wrap around' the ace.
explanations.B: These ranks are not actually in sequence; the ace doesn't bridge K and 2.
explanations.C: Correct. The ace is either high (A-K-Q-J-10) or low (A-2-3-4-5), never both at once.
explanations.D: Suit is irrelevant; the ranks simply don't form a straight.

questions.question: In poker, 'the nuts' refers to:
options: The worst hand at the table
The best possible hand on a given board

explanations.A: A full house may or may not be the nuts depending on the board.
explanations.B: The nuts is the best hand, not the worst.
explanations.C: Pocket aces are strong preflop but are not 'the nuts' on most boards.
explanations.D: Correct. The nuts is the strongest holding available given the community cards.

questions.question: Making three of a kind using a pocket pair plus one board card is often called:

explanations.A: Correct. Three of a kind from a pocket pair plus a matching board card is a 'set'.
explanations.B: A boat is a full house.
explanations.C: The wheel is the low straight.
explanations.D: A kicker is a side card used to break ties.

questions.question: Board A-8-5-3-2. You hold A-K, opponent holds A-Q. Who wins?

explanations.A: A queen kicker is lower than your king.
explanations.B: Correct. Both pair aces; your king kicker beats the queen.
explanations.C: The kickers differ, so the pot is not split.
explanations.D: Suit is irrelevant; the higher kicker wins.

questions.question: Two players both pair the board's king. One has a queen kicker, the other a jack. Winner?

explanations.A: A jack is lower than a queen.
explanations.B: Different kickers mean no split.
explanations.C: Correct. With equal pairs, the higher side card wins.
explanations.D: The board does not outrank a pair plus a kicker here.

questions.question: Board: 10♠ J♠ Q♠ K♠ A♠ and everyone checks down. What happens?
options: The player with another spade wins
The first player to act wins
Split pot — everyone plays the board

explanations.A: An extra spade can't beat a royal flush already on the board.
explanations.B: Hole cards are irrelevant when the board is the best possible hand.
explanations.C: Position never decides a showdown.
explanations.D: Correct. The board itself is a royal flush, the best hand for all remaining players.

questions.question: 'Playing the board' means:
options: Using zero of your hole cards because the five community cards are your best hand
Using both hole cards every hand

explanations.A: Correct. When the board beats anything your hole cards add, you play the board.
explanations.B: That is the opposite of playing the board.
explanations.C: Folding ends your involvement; it isn't playing the board.
explanations.D: That describes aggression, not playing the board.

questions.question: When two players have identical best five-card hands, the pot is:
options: Awarded to the player in earlier position
Carried over to the next hand

explanations.A: Position never breaks a showdown tie.
explanations.B: Correct. Equal hands chop the pot.
explanations.C: Betting order does not decide ties.
explanations.D: Tied pots are split immediately, not carried over.

questions.question: When two players both have a flush, the winner is decided by:
options: Who holds more cards of that suit

explanations.A: Both use exactly five cards; quantity beyond five is irrelevant.
explanations.B: Suits have no ranking value in Hold'em.
explanations.C: Correct. Compare the five flush cards from the top down.
explanations.D: Order of action does not break ties.

questions.question: Three hearts sit on the board and you hold no hearts. You:
options: Cannot make a flush from this board

explanations.A: A flush needs five cards of one suit; three on board plus none in hand isn't enough.
explanations.B: Holding no hearts does not win anything by itself.
explanations.C: You have no flush at all here.
explanations.D: Correct. Three hearts on the board is only three of a suit; without two hearts of your own you have no flush.

questions.question: Which of these is four of a kind?

explanations.A: Correct. Four nines with a king kicker.
explanations.B: That is a full house.
explanations.C: That is three of a kind.
explanations.D: That is a straight.

questions.question: Two players both have a straight. The winner is the one whose straight:

explanations.B: Correct. Compare the highest card of each straight.
explanations.C: A suited straight would be a straight flush; among plain straights, the top card decides.
explanations.D: Action order never decides a showdown.

questions.question: Two players both have three of a kind of different ranks. Who wins?

explanations.A: A higher set of trips wins outright regardless of kickers.
explanations.B: Different trip ranks do not tie.
explanations.C: Correct. For example, trip kings beat trip nines.
explanations.D: Betting history doesn't decide showdowns.

questions.question: On a board with three spades, the 'nut flush' is held by the player with:
